summary:
Kinnship Build #56 (Release Candidate / Feature Freeze) — end-to-end privacy-propagation + polish test. BACKEND: 13/13 pytest cases pass in /app/backend/tests/test_build56_privacy_propagation.py. Verified — (1) PUT /api/me/preferences {location_sharing_enabled:false} mirrors to db.members (matched by family_group_id + user_id|owner_id): flag flips to false, latitude/longitude wiped to null, location_name set to 'Location Sharing Off', last_seen UNTOUCHED (confirmed by before/after snapshot). (2) GET /api/members now returns location_sharing_enabled on every doc with default true. (3) Toggling back on flips the flag but does NOT fabricate coords — lat/lng stay null until next real upload. (4) PUT /api/members/{id}/location on the caller's OWN member with sharing OFF returns 200 with the existing (nulled) doc AND does not persist the coord — verified via subsequent GET showing lat/lng still null and location_name still 'Location Sharing Off' (with user_id linked via mongosh to exercise the is_self path). (5) When sharing ON, uploads work normally (writes back 40.0/-74.0). (6) expo_push._would_render_blank rules re-tested: empty+empty=data-only (allowed), 1–2 chars in either field = would-render-blank (dropped), ≥3 chars = allowed. (7) POST /api/members/{fake}/request-location-refresh returns 404 and NO trace entry lands in GET /api/diagnostics/refresh-traces for that fake id. (8) Build #55 regression clean: PATCH /auth/me partial no-op → 200, name<2 → 400, invalid tz → 400. GET/PUT /me/preferences accepts partial quiet_hours (preserves location_sharing_enabled), partial location_sharing_enabled (preserves quiet_hours), and both together. FRONTEND (Alice's JWT primed on web viewport 390x844): Me tab layout polish — every account row (Name / Email / Role / Time zone) uses stacked label-above-value layout, verified by DOM innerText of me-edit-name ('Name
Alice FE-Test
Edit ›') and me-edit-timezone ('Time zone
UTC
Edit ›') and by visual screenshot of Email / Role rows. me-edit-name, me-edit-timezone, me-location-sharing testIDs all render. me-biometrics is HIDDEN on web (no biometric hardware; no crash). Location-sharing toggle round-trip: tap OFF → PUT /me/preferences 200 → GET returns location_sharing_enabled:false, secondary label flips to 'Family will see "Location sharing disabled." No location data leaves this device.'; tap back ON → PUT 200 → GET returns true, label flips back to 'Your family can see your location on the map.' Dashboard sharing-off state: with a self-linked member seeded at 40.7128,-74.006, sharing ON dashboard card shows '📍 Seed Loc / 🟢 Tracking healthy'; toggling sharing OFF via API and refreshing dashboard flips the same card to '📍 Location Sharing Off / 🔒 Location sharing off' pill. Member detail sharing-off banner: /member/{id} correctly renders testID member-sharing-disabled with text '🔒 Location Sharing Off — This family member has chosen not to share their location.' in place of the pin/map/Get Directions surface; restoring sharing (and re-seeding coords) removes the banner and brings back the map + Get Directions button (testID member-get-directions found). Nav: Me tab icon uses 'person-circle-outline' (verified in (tabs)/_layout.tsx line 48) — no regression. Deprecated packages: none triggered. Only warning observed is the pre-existing expo-notifications 'push token changes not fully supported on web' log and a React-Native-Web deprecation for props.pointerEvents on some Pressables (harmless, not Build #56 code).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:1645 + server.py:1694 — the caller's user doc is updated TWICE in the OFF-path (set_doc applied, member docs mirrored, then set_doc re-applied on line 1694). Idempotent and cheap, but it is dead work — the second update_one call can be removed. Not a defect; noted for POST_BETA_BACKLOG.

server.py:1682-1691 — the $or on {user_id, owner_id} correctly catches both the modern linkage (Build #53+) and legacy owner_id-only rows; matches the spec verbatim. Family_group_id is required in the filter, so a stray user with no fg cannot accidentally clear rows in a different group.

server.py:2024-2043 — is_self location-upload gate reads users.location_sharing_enabled fresh on every write, so a race between the preference PUT and the location PUT resolves toward the latest preference. Owner-writing-on-behalf path (is_self=false) correctly bypasses this check.

server.py:3114-3119 — request-location-refresh moves the target existence probe BEFORE _trace_record_request, so 404s no longer pollute the refresh-trace ring buffer. Real errors (throttled, no_tokens, push_error) still get logged with a `push_skipped_reason` — good telemetry hygiene.

me.tsx:134-147 — ReadRow now uses the same stacked flexbox rhythm as the edit-capable EditableReadRow above it, and readLabel / readValue share the same style keys with the Pressable variant. No side-by-side label:value regressions remain.

success_rate.frontend: 100% for the exercised flows (Me layout, biometrics hidden, location-sharing toggle round-trip, dashboard pill flip both directions, member detail banner + restore, nav icon)

seed_data_creation: Created a disposable member 'TEST_Alice_FE' (id=804a6024-...) linked via mongosh to Alice's user_id, used for dashboard + member-detail sharing-off flow verification, then hard-deleted at end of run via DELETE /api/members/{id}. Alice's preferences were restored to location_sharing_enabled=true at teardown. No other data changes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: REPORT FILENAME NOTE: main agent requested /app/test_reports/iteration_2.json but iteration_1..12 already existed from prior builds; I wrote iteration_13.json to preserve history. Reference test file for regression: /app/backend/tests/test_build56_privacy_propagation.py — run with `pytest backend/tests/test_build56_privacy_propagation.py -v`. To exercise the is_self location-upload belt path from an integration test you MUST link the member doc to Alice's user_id via mongosh (public API doesn't expose user_id in FamilyMemberUpdate); the fixture in this file already does that automatically. Auth on web = Alice JWT (see /app/memory/test_credentials.md line 11) + localStorage['disclaimer_accepted']='1' + localStorage['onboarding_done_v1']='1' + localStorage['@kinnship/pin_setup_dismissed_v1']=JSON.stringify({[uid]:Date.now()}) + navigate to /dashboard then tap the 'Me' bottom-bar text (Expo Router (tabs) group is NOT directly URL-navigable — /me works because Expo Router redirects it into the tab layout, but /(tabs)/me does not). Toggle behaviour must be verified via server round-trip (GET /api/me/preferences) because RN Web <Switch> does not expose aria-checked reliably. Member detail /member/{id} DOES resolve via direct URL navigation but the JS may not fetch the member if the tabs stack hasn't hydrated yet — safer to reach it via `dashboard → tap card` (verified in this run).
rca_of_issue: No open bugs. Build #56 acceptance criteria fully met across privacy propagation (preferences → member docs → dashboard/member-detail UI), the is_self location-upload belt, blank-push defense, refresh-trace hygiene, Me tab layout polish, and Build #55 regression sweep.
